title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 红细胞向全身细胞递送氧气的过程
text_en: Then, the red blood cells deliver oxygen to cells throughout the body.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的遗传机制：父母双方遗传的缺陷基因导致β-珠蛋白基因突变，产生异常的血红蛋白S（HbS），HbS在红细胞内形成刚性链状结构，使红细胞变为镰刀形
text_en: In sickle cell anemia, there is a problem with your hemoglobin that makes your red blood cells sickle shaped instead of round. This is caused by a defective gene that is passed from both parents to children through genetic material in yourselves, called DNA. DNA is made up of sections called genes. Two of the genes called alfaglobin and beta-globin contain the blueprints for making the hemoglobin In sickle cell anemia, the beta-globin gene has changed or mutated. This mutation leads to the formation of an abnormal hemoglobin protein called hemoglobin S. Hemoglobin S forms into rigid strands inside your red blood cells, which causes the red blood cell to be sickle shaped.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞的病理后果：阻塞血流导致疼痛和组织损伤；镰状细胞寿命仅10-20天（正常为90-120天），身体无法及时补充被破坏的红细胞，导致贫血
text_en: Sickle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage. And while normal red blood cells last 90 to 120 days, sickle cells only last 10 to 20 Your body may not be able to keep up with how fast the cells are destroyed. As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal. This condition is called anemia.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的症状与并发症：症状包括手脚/腰背/胸腹疼痛、黄疸、疲劳；长期并发症包括眼、心、肺、肝、肾、关节的严重损害
text_en: The symptoms of sickle cell anemia vary from person to person, but may include episodes of pain in your body parts such as your hands and feet, lower back, chest, and abdomen. A yellow tint in the whites of the eyes and skin called jaundice and feeling tired due Over time sickle cell anemia can lead to complications such as severe pain and damage to your eyes, heart, lungs, liver, kidneys, and joints.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 脾脏损伤增加感染风险；高风险人群包括撒哈拉以南非洲、南美西班牙裔地区、加勒比海、中美洲、沙特阿拉伯及地中海国家的后裔
text_en: Damage to your spleen and organ that makes immune cells can increase your risk of infections. The risk for sickle cell anemia is highest among people with ancestors from sub-Saharan Africa, Hispanic areas of South America, the Caribbean and Central America, Saudi Arabia, and Mediterranean countries.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血症的基本病理：异常血红蛋白S使红细胞从柔软的圆盘形变为僵硬的镰刀形，导致血流阻塞、疼痛和组织损伤，且镰状细胞寿命短，造成贫血
text_en: Cycle cell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells. In this disease, an abnormal protein molecule called hemoglobin S changes your disc-shaped flexible red blood cells into rigid, sickle-shaped cells. Cycle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage. And sickle cells don't last as long as normal red blood cells. As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal. This condition is called anemia.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的治疗概述：包括药物治疗、医疗操作缓解症状，以及造血干细胞移植可能治愈疾病
text_en: Treatment options for sickle cell anemia may include medicines or procedures to help relieve symptoms, or in certain cases a hematopoid-extem cell transplant to potentially cure the disease.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 药物治疗方案：抗生素和疫苗预防感染，止痛药缓解疼痛，羟基脲（hydroxyurea）作为长期用药减少红细胞损伤和并发症，以及其他处方药物如crizanlizumab、voxelotor和L-谷氨酰胺
text_en: Antibiotics and vaccine shots for diseases can help prevent infections. Your healthcare provider may advise you to take pain relievers for severe or chronic pain. One of the most common medicines for long-term treatment of sickle cell anemia is called hydroxyuria. It helps reduce pain by preventing damage to your red blood cells. It also reduces the risk of long-term complications. Other prescribed medicines that help prevent complications and improve long-term outcomes include chrysanlysmab, buxelator, and elglutamine.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 输血治疗与造血干细胞移植适应症：严重疼痛发作时可进行输血替换镰状细胞；对于有并发症或高风险的年轻患者，建议进行造血干细胞移植
text_en: If you have an episode of very severe pain, you may have a procedure called a blood transfusion. This replaces the sickle cells with healthy red blood cells. For young patients who have, or are at risk for complications from sickle-cell anemia, a hematopoid-extem cell transplant may be advised.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 造血干细胞移植的具体过程：先用化疗药物和/或放射治疗摧毁骨髓中的异常造血干细胞，再从组织配型匹配的供体（通常是家族成员）获取健康干细胞，输入后干细胞迁移至骨髓生成正常红细胞
text_en: It can cure some people who have this disease. For the transplant, you may be given chemotherapy drugs, radiation therapy, or both. This will destroy the abnormal blood-forming stem cells in your bone marrow. Then, you will receive healthy stem cells from either the bone marrow or blood of a donor. The donor is often a family member whose tissue type closely matches yours. The healthy blood-forming stem cells from the donor will travel through your blood to your bone marrow, where they will make normal red blood cells instead of sickle cells.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣的正常解剖与功能：主动脉瓣位于左心室与主动脉之间，由三个杯状瓣叶组成。左心室收缩时血液通过瓣叶泵入主动脉，舒张时瓣膜关闭防止血液回流。
text_en: Your aortic valve is one of the four valves in your heart. It lies between your left ventricle or lower left chamber and the large blood vessel supplying oxygen-rich blood to your body called the aorta. When your left ventricle contracts during each heartbeat, it pushes blood through three flexible cup-like leaflets that make up your aortic valve. When your left ventricle relaxes, your aortic valve closes to prevent blood from flowing back into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 钙化性主动脉瓣狭窄的病理过程：随年龄增长，瓣叶出现钙质沉积，变厚变硬，无法完全开放，导致瓣口狭窄、血流减少，左心室壁因代偿性增厚而肥大。
text_en: As you get older, your aortic valve may develop deposits of calcium that cause your leaflets to get thicker and less flexible. In this condition, your leaflets may not open fully. This condition, called calcific aortic valve stenosis, creates a narrowed opening that reduces the amount of blood flowing into your aorta and out to your body. As a result, the wall of your left ventricle thickens because it has to work harder to pump blood through the narrowed valve opening.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 手术入路与导丝置入：在腹股沟做小切口，经股动脉插入引导鞘管，通过鞘管送入柔性导丝，导丝沿股动脉上行至主动脉，穿过主动脉瓣进入左心室。
text_en: At the beginning of your procedure, your doctor will make a small incision in your groin. After creating an opening in a blood vessel, called the femoral artery, your doctor will insert a flexible tube called an introducer sheath. Through the sheath, your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into your femoral artery. The guide wire will be passed through your femoral artery all the way up to your aorta. Then the guide wire will be pushed through the opening in your aortic valve and into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 球囊预扩张：沿导丝送入带球囊的导管穿过主动脉瓣，充盈球囊扩张狭窄的瓣口并将原有瓣叶推向两侧，随后回缩球囊并撤出导管。
text_en: Your doctor will use the guide wire to pass a flexible tube called a catheter through your aortic valve. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be inflated to widen the opening in your aortic valve to push the valve leaflets to the sides. After deflating the balloon, your doctor will remove the catheter.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 人工瓣膜植入：替换瓣膜由组织瓣和金属支架组成，被压缩后装载于另一根导管尖端，送至主动脉瓣位置后充盈球囊使瓣膜及支架展开就位，支架固定瓣膜，随后撤出球囊导管和导丝。
text_en: The replacement valve consists of a tissue valve surrounded by a wire mesh called a stent. The replacement valve will be compressed and placed on the tip of another catheter. This catheter will be inserted through your aortic valve. When the catheter reaches the opening in your aortic valve, your doctor will inflate a balloon underneath the replacement valve to expand it and the stent. After placement of the new aortic valve, your doctor will deflate the balloon. The stent will support and secure the replacement valve in place. The catheter and guide wire will be removed.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 手术结束与切口关闭：撤出引导鞘管并缝合腹股沟切口；对侧腹股沟用于术中心脏监测的器械切口也一并缝合关闭。
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the introducer sheath in your groin will be removed and your skin incision will be closed. You will have another incision on the other side of your groin through which instruments were inserted to monitor your heart during the procedure. This skin incision will also be closed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣的解剖位置与正常功能：二尖瓣位于左心房与左心室之间，控制含氧血液从左心房流入左心室，心室收缩时瓣膜关闭防止血液回流
text_en: Your mitral valve is one of the four valves in your heart. It lies between your left atrium or upper chamber and left ventricle or lower chamber. Oxygen-rich blood coming from your lungs goes into the left atrium. When your left atrium contracts, blood flows through your open mitral valve and into your left ventricle. When your left ventricle contracts, your mitral valve closes to prevent blood from flowing back into your left atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣的结构：二尖瓣由前叶和后叶两片瓣叶组成，腱索将瓣叶连接至左心室内壁，辅助防止血液反流
text_en: Your mitral valve has two leaflets, an anterior or front leaflet, and a posterior or back String-like tissue, called tendinous cords, attach both leaflets to the inside of your left ventricle. The tendinous cords help prevent blood from flowing back into your atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣狭窄：瓣膜开口过窄导致从左心房流向左心室的血流量减少，病因包括风湿热和先天性心脏缺陷
text_en: You may have one or both of the two main problems that can happen to your mitral valve. One problem is stenosis, in which the opening in your mitral valve is two narrow. The narrow opening reduces the amount of blood flowing through the valve from your left atrium to your left ventricle. Your mental valve stenosis may be caused by rheumatic fever or heart-birth defects.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣关闭不全（反流）：瓣膜无法完全关闭导致血液回流至左心房，病因包括二尖瓣脱垂、风湿性心脏病、先天缺陷及心脏扩张；狭窄和反流均加重心脏负担
text_en: The other problem your valve may have is regurgitation, in which your mitral valve doesn't close completely. Retraction allows blood to leak back into your left atrium when your left ventricle contracts. Mitral valve regurgitation may be caused by mitral valve prolapse, in which the valve leaflets and cords become weak and loose. Rheumatic heart disease, which may result from rheumatic fever, heart-birth defects, or heart enlargement, called dilation, which may result from a heart attack. Both stenosis and regurgitation strain your heart because it has to pump harder to move oxygen-rich blood to your body.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路、预防性使用抗生素、全身麻醉、插入气管导管辅助呼吸
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started. You may be given antibiotics through the IV to decrease your chance of infection. You will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free during the A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and down your throat to help you breathe during the procedure.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 机器人手术系统操作：在右侧胸壁做4-5个小切口，插入内窥镜和机器人手臂工具，工具可360度旋转；外科医生通过操控台的操纵杆和脚踏板控制机器人臂，计算机精确转译手部动作，提供放大的三维立体视野
text_en: Your surgeon will typically make four or five small incisions on the right side of your An assistant will insert a special camera called an endoscope and other tools attached to the robotic arms through the incisions. Unlike standard laparoscopic instruments, these tools can rotate 360 degrees and have more flexibility than the human wrist. Seated at a special console, your surgeon will operate the robotic arms and the camera with joystick-like controls and foot pedals. A computer will translate the exact movements of your surgeon's hands into precise movements of the surgical tools. Your surgeon will have a magnified three-dimensional stereoscopic view inside your body through a high-definition vision system.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 心脏手术核心步骤：连接体外循环机接管心肺功能并停跳心脏，切开左心房暴露二尖瓣，切断瓣膜与心脏的连接并移除病变瓣膜
text_en: After the robotic arms are in place, you will be placed on a heart-long machine. This machine will take over the functions of your heart and lungs while your heart is stopped for your operation. Your surgeon will begin the procedure on your heart by making an incision in your left atrium to get to your mitral valve. Next, all of your mitral valve's attachments to your heart will be cut and the valve will be removed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 人工瓣膜选择：机械瓣可终身使用但需终身服用抗凝药物；生物组织瓣（人或动物组织）寿命可能较短但无需抗凝治疗
text_en: Your damaged mitral valve may be replaced with an artificial mechanical valve. A mechanical mitral valve will last a lifetime. However, they require you to take blood thinner medication for the rest of your life. Alternatively, your mitral valve may be replaced with a valve made from human or animal tissue. A tissue mitral valve may not last as long as a mechanical valve. However, the natural tissue does not require blood thinner medication.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 手术收尾：植入新瓣膜后恢复心跳、撤除体外循环，用缝线、吻合钉、医用胶或皮肤闭合条关闭切口
text_en: Once your new valve is in place, your surgeon will restart the beating of your heart and remove the attachments to the heart-long machine. At the end of your procedure, your skin incisions will be closed with stitches, staples, surgical glue, or skin closure strips.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术后护理与出院：拔除气管导管、转入ICU监护、给予镇痛药及静脉输液、机械瓣患者使用抗凝药，大多数患者术后约一周出院
text_en: After your procedure, your breathing tube will be removed and you will be taken to the intensive care unit or ICU for monitoring. You will be given pain medication as needed. You may continue to receive fluids and medications through your IV. You will be given blood thinner medication if you have a mechanical valve. Most patients are released from the hospital about a week after the procedure.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化概述：一种可能从儿童期开始发展的危及生命的疾病，脂肪物质（斑块）在动脉壁内堆积，减少或完全阻断血流，严重时可导致心脏病发作或中风。
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood. This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow. In extreme cases, this disease can lead to a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化的危险因素：包括高血压、高胆固醇、吸烟、糖尿病、肥胖和缺乏运动。
text_en: These factors for atherosclerosis include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, tobacco smoke, diabetes, obesity, and physical inactivity.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 病变起始阶段：动脉内壁（内皮层）受损后，血液中的胆固醇、脂肪和细胞废物等物质在受损区域逐渐积聚。
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium. Over time, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 炎症反应与泡沫细胞形成：胆固醇分子氧化引发炎症反应，内皮细胞释放化学信号，单核细胞从血流中迁移至损伤处，在氧化胆固醇刺激下转化为巨噬细胞，巨噬细胞吞噬胆固醇后变成泡沫细胞，聚集形成斑块。
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build-up of material cause cholesterol molecules to This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site, release chemicals that signal a call for help. In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damage site. Demulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages. The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules. As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to form plaque.
